records.ety: [OE. brusen, brisen, brosen, bresen, AS. br<?/san or fr. OF. bruiser, bruisier, bruser, to break, shiver, perh. from OHG. brochisōn. Cf. Break, v. t.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Bruised (<?/); p. pr. & vb. n. Bruising.]

defs: To injure, as by a blow or collision, without laceration; to contuse; as, to bruise one's finger with a hammer; to bruise the bark of a tree with a stone; to bruise an apple by letting it fall.
To break; as in a mortar; to bray, as minerals, roots, etc.; to crush.
quotes: Nor bruise her flowerets with the armed hoofs.

defs: To fight with the fists; to box.
quotes: Bruising was considered a fine, manly, old English custom.

defs: An injury to the flesh of animals, or to plants, fruit, etc., with a blunt or heavy instrument, or by collision with some other body; a contusion; as, a bruise on the head; bruises on fruit.
quotes: From the sole of the foot even unto the head there is no soundness in it; but wounds, and bruises.
